explicit_text: In 1998, my father was killed by Burmese soldiers in the forest. He was an innocent villager who did nothing wrong, yet he lost his life because of the ongoing conflict. This tragic event deeply affected me and my family, shaping my view of the civil war in Burma. Despite this, I have never sought revenge but believe in treating others as I wish to be treated.
implicit_text: In 1998, my father was killed by government troops in the forest. He was an innocent villager who did nothing wrong, yet he lost his life because of the ongoing conflict. This tragic event deeply affected me and my family, shaping my view of the civil war in my country. Despite this, I have never sought revenge but believe in treating others as I wish to be treated.

explicit_text: Burma is my homeland, but it has been unstable due to the revolution between the government and ethnic groups like the Karen people. Growing up there was difficult, especially after my father died and my mother struggled to support us. Because of the civil war and lack of resources, I couldn't continue my education in Burma and eventually had to leave to find better opportunities.
implicit_text: My homeland has been unstable due to the revolution between the government and ethnic groups like the Karen people. Growing up there was difficult, especially after my father died and my mother struggled to support us. Because of the civil war and lack of resources, I couldn't continue my education in that country and eventually had to leave to find better opportunities.

explicit_text: I spent thirteen years living in refugee camps along the Thai-Burma border to pursue a better education. These camps were temporary settlements for people like me who were suffering from war and displacement. Although life there was difficult and unstable, it gave me a chance to learn and prepare for a future beyond the conflict.
implicit_text: I spent thirteen years living in refugee camps along the border between Thailand and my homeland to pursue a better education. These camps were temporary settlements for people like me who were suffering from war and displacement. Although life there was difficult and unstable, it gave me a chance to learn and prepare for a future beyond the conflict.

explicit_text: I immigrated to the United States in September 2014 to build a new life filled with peace and freedom. Here, I have learned the importance of education, money, and time, which are powerful tools for success. The U.S. offers a different government, living standards, and opportunities compared to Burma, and I am hopeful that my life will be more comfortable while still remembering my Karen roots and their ongoing struggle.
implicit_text: I immigrated to North America in September 2014 to build a new life filled with peace and freedom. Here, I have learned the importance of education, money, and time, which are powerful tools for success. This country offers a different government, living standards, and opportunities compared to my homeland, and I am hopeful that my life will be more comfortable while still remembering my Karen roots and their ongoing struggle.
